expectations.evidence: The response opens with 'Create a feature, not a column.' and ends with 'Use a feature.' — unambiguous recommendation throughout.

Explicitly mentions provenance tracking ('Who scored it — the Execution links to the workflow and the user who ran it'), multiple scores from different scorers ('each technician's execution produces its own row; no overwriting'), and controlled vocabulary ('quality values are stored in a vocabulary table, so you can enforce valid values'). All three criteria are covered.
Step 3 and 4 create an 'Image_Quality_Score' vocabulary with terms Good, Adequate, and Poor — quality-related terms appropriate to fundus image rating.

The response explains 'each score is tied to an Execution record' providing who scored it and when, and the 'How Technicians Record Scores' section describes one execution per technician per review session so every score row carries the Execution RID linking score to technician identity.

The response explicitly lists why a column falls short ('No record of who scored it or when', 'This does not meet your stated requirements') and never recommends add_column as a solution. The entire recommendation is to use a feature.
